subject: Cumulative and Survival Distribution Functions
question: Let $X$ have probability mass function (pmf) $\Pr(x) = \frac{1}{8}$ for $x = 1, 2, \ldots, 8$. Find (a) the cumulative distribution function (cdf) of $X$; (b) $\Pr(X > 5)$.
explanation: (a) The cdf is given by $F(x) = \begin{cases} 0 & x < 1 \\ \frac{\lfloor x\rfloor}{8} & 1\le x\le 8 \\ 1 & x > 8 \end{cases}$ where $\lfloor x\rfloor$ is the floor function of $x$. (b) We have $\Pr(X > 5) = 1-F(5) = 1-\frac{5}{8} = \frac{3}{8}$. Proposition 25.6 For any random variable $X$ and any real number $a$, we have $\Pr(X < a) = \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(a-\frac{1}{n}\right) = F(a^-)$. Proof. For each positive integer $n$, define $E_n = \{s\in S : X(s)\le a-\frac{1}{n}\}$. Then $\{E_n\}$ is an increasing sequence of sets such that $\bigcup_{n=1}^\infty E_n = \{s\in S : X(s) < a\}$. We have $\Pr(X < a) = \Pr\left(\lim_{n\to\infty}\left\{X\le a-\frac{1}{n}\right\}\right) = \lim_{n\to\infty} \Pr\left(X\le a-\frac{1}{n}\right) = \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(a-\frac{1}{n}\right) = F(a^-)$. Note that $\Pr(X < a)$ does not necessarily equal $F(a)$, since $F(a)$ also includes the probability that $X$ equals $a$. Corollary 25.1 $\Pr(X\ge a) = 1-\lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(a-\frac{1}{n}\right) = 1-F(a^-)$. Proposition 25.7 If $a < b$ then $\Pr(a < X\le b) = F(b)-F(a)$. Proof. Let $A = \{s : X(s) > a\}$ and $B = \{s : X(s)\le b\}$. Note that $\Pr(A\cup B) = 1$. Then $\Pr(a < X\le b) = \Pr(A\cap B) = \Pr(A) + \Pr(B) - \Pr(A\cup B) = (1-F(a)) + F(b) - 1 = F(b)-F(a)$. Proposition 25.8 If $a < b$ then $\Pr(a\le X < b) = F(b^-)-F(a^-)$. Proof. Let $A = \{s : X(s)\ge a\}$ and $B = \{s : X(s) < b\}$. Note that $\Pr(A\cup B) = 1$. We have $\Pr(a\le X < b) = \Pr(A\cap B) = \Pr(A) + \Pr(B) - \Pr(A\cup B) = \left(1-\lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(a-\frac{1}{n}\right)\right) + \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(b-\frac{1}{n}\right) - 1 = \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(b-\frac{1}{n}\right) - \lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(a-\frac{1}{n}\right) = F(b^-)-F(a^-)$. Proposition 25.9 If $a < b$ then $\Pr(a\le X\le b) = F(b)-\lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(a-\frac{1}{n}\right) = F(b)-F(a^-)$. Proof. Let $A = \{s : X(s)\ge a\}$ and $B = \{s : X(s)\le b\}$. Note that $\Pr(A\cup B) = 1$. Then $\Pr(a\le X\le b) = \Pr(A\cap B) = \Pr(A) + \Pr(B) - \Pr(A\cup B) = \left(1-\lim_{n\to\infty} F\left(a-\frac{1}{n}\right)\right) + F(b) - 1 = F(b)-F(a^-)$
